title: Ceratolobus laevigatus
intro: Ceratolobus laevigatus, commonly known as the Malayan calabash, is a plant species belonging to the family Achariaceae. The plant is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Ceratolobus laevigatus is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being used in folk remedies for its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Ceratolobus laevigatus is a species of plant in the family Arecaceae. It is a type of palm tree native to Borneo. The classification of Ceratolobus laevigatus includes being a member of the genus Ceratolobus. Within the Arecaceae family, it is more specifically categorized as being part of the subfamily Calamoideae. Further classification places it within the tribe Calameae.
